ELR Name: CATHCART CIRCLE (MUIRHOUSE NORTH JN - MUIRHOUSE CENTRAL JN)
Free Text: Buried Power cable 240/415v originates from internal supply at Cathcart ECR NGRS 258738-660846 at the 0m 0440 yards. Cable first enters trough route for 30m, then via a URX for 13m, then via trough route for 160m, then enters GRP <plastic troughing> route for 6m climbs banking to meet trough route at the main lines, the cable enters this route and proceeds within for the next 100m, the cable then crosses the both lines via UTX for 12m, then back into trough route for 67m towards REB, finally on approach to REB it goes via buried trough for 5m and then via surface trough route for 7m to rear of REB. Total length of 400m. REB located at 258610 - 660925 at the 1M 1699 Yards point.

Free Text: This water and wall calcification <simlair to stalagtites> in the tunnel area have been taken for micro-biological analysis. Two reports concluded "Due to the negative results for the Coliform and E. coli bacteria and general low bacteria levels detected in the water ingress, there is a low risk from bacteriological hazards to staff working in the tunnel. Based on the results obtained from this assessment, staff working within the system should wear appropriate PPE including disposable gloves and coveralls to minimise any direct contact. It is presumed that standard eye protection and hard hats will be worn as a minimum requirement for any task which should be suitable in respect for reducing contact with the water ingress."  More details on the reports can be found by contacting Glasgow Delivery Unit WHSEA.

Local Name: Corkerhill Carriage Maintenance and Depot Sidings
Free Text: Before setting up a safe system of work within the confines of Corkerhill CSMD <Carriage Sidings and Maintenance Depot>  the PICOS <Person In Charge Of a Sidings> shall meet with the Yard Coordinator. Full discussion and agreement shall be reached following Abellio ScotRail"s process MAIF007CK - Record of line Possession or blockage. Form MAI/F/007/CK shall be completed with the Yard Coordinator and PICOS . Staff cannot go on or near the line in Corkerhill CSMD area until full agreement has been reached with the Yard Coordinator and PICOS and all safety protocols have been put in place for staff to remain safe from the passage of trains. Please contact Glasgow WHSEA for more assistance.

ELR Name: DRUMGELLOCH - HELENSBURGH (VIA SINGER) COM HIGH ST STATION -K'WOOD SOUTH JN
Free Text:
Pantograph Monitoring System cameras have been installed at Hyndland Station on the footbridge and will be working from 26th October 2019.  The camera is located on the footbridge and contains lasers to be able to scan the pantograph.  The cameras contains 2 types of lasers: ~
LDS30 Laser Ranger ~
- The laser rangers are used for distance measurements and for identifying when a train/pantograph is under the scanner. ~
- The laser rangers are Laser Class 1 and herefore considered eye safe under all conditions. ~
ZQ1 Line Laser ~
- The line laser emits a laser line with an angel of 60 degrees.~
- The line laser is not to be considered eye safe at the emitting point of the laser.  However since the laser spreads its energy in an angle, the intensity per area decreases with the distance. ~
It has been agreed that when work is taking place using machines underneath the cameras the cameras should be turned off.  This can be done using the isolation switch on structure number B 03 42

Free Text: SCOTLAND ROUTE HAS INTRODUCED A PROHIBITION ON THE USE OF WARNING SSOW WORKING UNLESS PROTECTION IS PROVIDED BY LOWS OR TOWS. THIS INCLUDES WORKING AS AN INDIVIDUAL WORKING ALONE OR GOING DOWN THE HIERARCHY WHEN PROTECTION SSOW IS OR BECOMES UNAVAILABLE. YOU WILL STILL BE ALLOWED TO "CROSS THE LINE" OR GO ROUND AN OBSTACLE AS LONG AS YOUR PRIMARY SSOW IS A PROTECTION SSOW AND YOU HAVE SIGHTING DISTANCE AND HAVE A WAY OF CALCULATING THIS DISTANCE. DO NOT ACCEPT ANY PLAN THAT HAS WARNING SSOW AS THE PRIMARY SSOW UNLESS LOWS OR TOWS ARE UTILISED.

Free Text: Following on from the recent various RRV derailments on the Tulloch sidings Scotland Route are restricting the use until further notice of lighter weight RRVs such as Landrovers, Rail Rangers, Nissan Navarro?s, which are all under 3.5tonne from on/off tracking in the sidings.This restriction does not apply top  larger heavier weight RRVs such as Iveco?s, Canters and Unimogs etc or 360 degree excavators and trailers, also the sleeper suite RMMMs including the Brad mini tamper, MRT sleeper changers and the Robel Mini tamper.OTM tampers or stone blowers are not affected by this restriction on using the sidingsAny issues or further clarification please contact DL-RSCOPlant@networkrail.co.uk

Free Text: SCOTLAND ROUTE HAS INTRODUCED A PROHIBITION ON THE USE OF WARNING SSOW WORKING UNLESS PROTECTION IS PROVIDED BY LOWS OR TOWS. THIS INCLUDES WORKING AS AN INDIVIDUAL WORKING ALONE OR GOING DOWN THE HIERARCHY WHEN PROTECTION SSOW IS OR BECOMES UNAVAILABLE. YOU WILL STILL BE ALLOWED TO "CROSS THE LINE" OR GO ROUND AN OBSTACLE AS LONG AS YOUR PRIMARY SSOW IS A PROTECTION SSOW AND YOU HAVE SIGHTING DISTANCE AND HAVE A WAY OF CALCULATING THIS DISTANCE. DO NOT ACCEPT ANY PLAN THAT HAS WARNING SSOW AS THE PRIMARY SSOW UNLESS LOWS OR TOWS ARE UTILISED.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: All tracks have overhead 25,000-volt AC electric traction supply cables. All works undertaken on the station must be carried out in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified lines

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Requirement to disturb the surface within the station's controlled area, the Buried Services Procedure shall be followed at all times. Work will not be allowed to commence until such time that a Permit to Dig/Penetrate form has been duly signed by the Contract Administrator. Numerous low voltage cables are located underground throughout the station. Each contractor will be responsible for the scanning of the work site in order to locate the exact location of the underground power cables.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: In addition to the above cabling, Glasgow Central Station contains a large quantity of operational equipment including Signalling and Telecommunications <S&T> cables and train dispatch equipment. Any contractor proposing to undertake any demolition, excavation or works that could disturb any cables should refer to the National Hazard Directory. Additional information on S&T equipment can be obtained from the Route Signalling Engineer.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Wayleave for cables - Midland Street 15. In addition to the above information there are underground cables located at the following location: Midland Street 15 - U/G Cable in roadway. Wayleave ref. GLC01601 <which can be found in CCMS>, old number No. 99040/4019/000

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Wayleave for cables - Union Street 39/49. In addition to the above information there are underground cables located at the following location: Union Street 39/49 rear - U/G L/T. Wayleave ref. ZSS10439 <which can be found on CCMS>, old number No. 99040/5191/000

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Limited records of the drainage layout to the station are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that drainage exists beneath platforms, concourse and in the arch complex.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Records of the water supply pipeline layouts although limited are indicative of the main runs and are held by Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. The main water supply valves are located in Hope Street basement <access via the 'Old Lost Property store'> and the main feeder tanks with separate meter supply for the train watering system, are located in the Broomielaw arches.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Records of the gas supply pipeline layouts are indicative of the main runs and are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. The main supply enters the station via the Red Star archway on Hope Street and is piped to the boiler room in archway 4E. There is also a gas supply <serving Burger King> off Union Street Ramp. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency contact 0800 111 999. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Within the Arches beneath the platforms there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin. This is particularly the case in those arches which are currently unused and contain large amounts of rubbish, thus providing a potential breeding ground. Adjacent to `The Arches? <south> is a lane with a number of arches used for storage purposes. This area is known to be frequented by vagrants, prostitutes and drug addicts. Care is to be taken to avoid contact with hypodermic syringes. Contractors should be aware that poison has been put down at various locations.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Within the voids beneath the platforms there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin. This is particularly the case in those arches which are currently unused and contain large amounts of rubbish, thus providing a potential breeding ground. Adjacent to `The Arches? <south> is a lane with a number of arches used for storage purposes. This area is known to be frequented by vagrants, prostitutes and drug addicts. Care is to be taken to avoid contact with hypodermic syringes. Contractors should be aware that poison has been put down at various locations.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: No section of Glasgow Central Station is classed as a sub surface station. It should however be noted that Glasgow Central Low Level Station <that extends to the top of the escalators at the main station concourse> is designated a sub surface station. This station is operated and managed by First ScotRail.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Arch E4 - main boiler room. This is the main boiler room and houses 4 gas boilers and a secluded small control room. A room to the rear of this houses the standby diesel generator. The location of the gas pipework has not been indicated and surveys have identified asbestos in some areas, please see ARMS for more details.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Arch E5 - HV switch room. This area is the high voltage compound and access is restricted to authorised persons with a Level C card. It is believed that asbestos is present in the cladding of walls above head height, please see ARMS for details.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Lower Sub-Basement. Back of hoist no 63 - caged HV equipment. This caged of HV equipment is placed in a corner of this big open corridor room and must only be accessed by authorised personnel.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Platform 6/7 - hoist no 62. This must only be used by authorised personnel.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Platform 10/11 - hoist no 63. This must only be used by authorised personnel. Access to the hoist mechanism is via a staircase. The archway leads to the fire exit to the low level station. There are manhole covers on the floor and switchboard and cables/pipe work on the walls. Since the manhole covers could not be removed, they should be considered as limited access / confined space unless discovered otherwise. The hazards within the sub basement include electrical, biological, falls and asbestos <please see ARMS for details>.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Basement/Arch 10 East - hoist no 60. Hoist No 60 gives access to platform 1. Also in this area is the lift room and a plant room. Throughout this part of the basement, there are numerous pipe work, cables, ducting, and manholes.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Arch 4E - standby generator. There is a standby generator located in Arch 4E which provides limited emergency supplies to the station in the event of a power failure.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Access to the station roof is via the lift or stairs on platforms 9 and 10. Contractors working on the roof should be careful not to disturb existing glazing and should notify the SSM of any damaged glass discovered, ensuring compliance with Safe System of Work. Contractors should also be mindful of objects which may be thrown from the windows of the adjoining hotel.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Lower Arches <south> & Retail accom - Switch Room. The switch room is accessed from the empty arch next to the First Scotrail car park at Midland Street. In this area the lighting and switchboard are fairly high and the floor is uneven in areas.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Lower Arches <central>/Arches Theatre - Pump room. Towards the back of the furthest arch `Devils Arch? within Arches Theatre is the Pump Room. The room giving access to the pump Room has an uneven floor with piles of sand and gravel and the lighting does not function. The Pump Room itself is well lit but includes many hazards. There are two deep shafts, one with a winch and apparatus and the other is an access to the lower pump area. This is fitted with a ladder and is undoubtedly a confined space. Another main hazard is the sump area which receives and redirects the water. These areas are fitted with barriers. There are two floor levels, floor slabs which are believed to cover cables, further electric cables and a switchboard. Waste materials and other materials, which may not be waste, litter the area. The hazards are electrical, slips, trips, falls, drowning and others associated with confined spaces.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L. (ELR CHANGE LAW JN - GLASGOW CENTRAL STATION)
Free Text: Basement - Main central corridor. This area of the arches has been partly upgraded with the installation of new lighting and heating and ventilation piping. The main electricity cables have also been labelled identifying them. Along the central area of the corridor runs metal covers which give access to a channel which houses high voltage cables. There are a number of locations within the corridor where cables run from the walls towards this channel. Areas within this channel will be considered as a confined space / limited access if working under the metal covers.
